item.title: Actis Raises $2.5 Billion at Energy Fund’s First Close
item.description: Actis has raised $2.5 billion at first close for its flagship energy fund, putting it about 40% of the way to its goal, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: US Funding Markets Are Flooded With Cash That’s Here to Stay
item.description: An abundance of cash in US funding markets appears to be driven by deeper structural shifts that are unlocking billions of dollars in balance-sheet capacity at the biggest banks, Wall Street strategists say.

item.title: Fed’s Williams Says Unclear How Productivity Boom Impacts Rates
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said it’s unclear how a lift in productivity will ultimately impact interest rates and the central bank’s policymaking.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Tick Up to 215,000, Highest in a Month
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits rose slightly, while remaining at a level consistent with a stable labor market.

item.title: US Consumer Spending Edges Up as Inflation Accelerates
item.description: US consumer spending edged up in April, with annual inflation accelerating to the highest level since 2023 as the Iran war drove up energy prices.

item.title: European Banks Could Cut 20% of Jobs on AI, Morgan Stanley Says
item.description: The rapid spread of artificial intelligence may enable European banks to reduce their headcount by as much as a fifth over the “shorter term,” according to Morgan Stanley analysts.

item.title: MetLife Clashes With Ares Over Struggling Football Investment
item.description: Two major financial firms have clashed over the restructuring of Eagle Football, the group that holds stakes in a number of football teams including the storied French club Olympique Lyonnais.

item.title: Apple’s iOS 27 Siri Overhaul and AI Features Previewed in New Images
item.description: The iPhone maker looks to stage a comeback in digital assistants and artificial intelligence.

item.title: BCI Expands Into Financing Private Equity Funds Amid Deal Slump
item.description: British Columbia Investment Management Corp. created a team within its private equity unit to provide financing to buyout firms that are increasingly looking for new ways to drum up cash amid a prolonged dealmaking drought.

item.title: Oura Ring 5 Is 40% Smaller, Detects Blood Pressure Changes and Sleep Disturbances
item.description: Oura Health Oy, the popular smart ring maker seeking to go public this year, unveiled a significantly thinner and lighter new model along with new wellness features.

item.title: AI’s Grip on Emerging Markets Fuels Rise in Stock-Picking ETFs
item.description: Large asset managers are rolling out a wave of actively managed emerging-market ETFs, pitching them as alternatives to benchmarks increasingly dominated by AI stocks.

item.title: Architect Set to Launch US-Listed Compute Futures in Challenge to CME, NYSE-owner
item.description: Architect Financial Technologies, an offshore derivatives exchange operator, has bought a US-regulated trading venue as it seeks to launch a futures market in computing power and other artificial intelligence commodities.

item.title: Boston Seizes on California Billionaire Tax to Lure AI Talent
item.description: The talent at some of America’s hottest artificial intelligence companies often passes through Boston-area universities before heading west to build billion-dollar businesses in Silicon Valley. Massachusetts business and political leaders say California’s proposed tax on billionaires is an opportunity to change that.

item.title: Goldman Traders See a Short Squeeze Brewing for Unloved Sectors
item.description: Stock-market bulls in the US may have an unlikely ally to thank for the next leg up: Short sellers.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 28, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.3% as of 7:32 a.m. in New York after attacks in the Middle East fueled doubts over whether an end to the war is imminent.

item.title: Russia and Kazakhstan Sign Energy, FX Swap Deals as Putin Visits
item.description: Russia and Kazakhstan signed a series of deals during a visit by President Vladimir Putin, including a currency swap arrangement and an agreement on financing for the construction of the Central Asian nation’s first nuclear power plant.

item.title: German Power Prices Swing From Zero to Nearly €400 in a Day
item.description: German power prices will swing sharply on Thursday, with electricity trading close to zero at noon before surging to nearly €400 a megawatt-hour in the evening as solar generation fades.

item.title: NextEra Is Betting Its Dominion Deal Can Speed US Infrastructure for AI
item.description: Extra heft may help the utilities break through the infrastructure logjam.

item.title: Ambani Says Reliance Evaluating ‘Strategic Pathways’ for Jio, No Word on IPO
item.description: Reliance Industries Ltd. is taking “deliberate steps” to strengthen its digital unit, Chairman Mukesh Ambani said in a note to shareholders, without sharing any updates on the planned listing of Jio Platforms Ltd. that is expected to fetch as much as $4 billion.

item.title: Caesars Agrees to Be Taken Over by Fertitta in $5.7 Billion Deal
item.description: Caesars Entertainment Inc. has agreed to be bought by Fertitta Entertainment Inc. in a $5.7 billion, all-cash deal that stands to form a massive entertainment empire in the US.

item.title: Meta Eyes AI Subscriptions While AI Rivals Target Meta’s Ad Business
item.description: The social giant wants to make money from its chatbot, but its core business may be at risk.

item.title: South Korea Grapples With an AI Boom Spurring $340,000 Bonuses
item.description: Policymakers in South Korea are grappling with how to manage the AI-fueled chip boom.

item.title: Mexico’s Record Exports to the US Show an Underlying Shift
item.description: Trump’s trade threats are stirring uncertainty over USMCA negotiations this year

item.title: After Rich Colleges Raid Basketball Team, School Exits D1 Sports
item.description: Saint Francis University was out of its league, and Father Malachi Van Tassell knew it.

item.title: Universal’s First Kid-Focused Theme Park to Open in Texas on July 1
item.description: Comcast Corp.’s Universal division will open its first theme park aimed specifically at young children this summer in an effort to reach a demographic that may be overwhelmed by some of the attractions at its bigger parks.

item.title: AT1 Market Is So Hot That Banks Are Locking In Rates for Decade
item.description: European lenders are selling Additional Tier 1 bonds with long call dates at a record pace, seizing ultra-favorable conditions in the market for risky bank debt to fix costs for a decade.

item.title: UK Could Miss Housing Target by 40% or More, Research Finds
item.description: The UK could miss its goal of adding 1.5 million new homes in the current parliamentary term by at least 40%, according to a report by Bloomberg Intelligence that urges the government to ramp up efforts to stimulate demand and help developers.

item.title: The AI Boom Is Warping the San Francisco Property Market
item.description: Ordinary renters are getting squeezed out.

item.title: Micron’s Rally to $1 Trillion Has Actual Earnings Behind It
item.description: Still, investors are skeptical those earnings are durable.

item.title: Senegal’s Looming Bond Payments Are Next Test Ahead of IMF Talks
item.description: Senegal has two interest payments on foreign-currency bonds due next month and while analysts expect them to proceed, they come at a tense political time in the West African nation ahead of talks with the International Monetary Fund.
